trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at 3.21ms (score 60.1) with detailed profiling: 37.4% elementwise copies, 29.1% GEMM, 11.3% elementwise mul, 6.8% exp, 5.9% masked_fill. Multiple failed approaches documented. Ready for optimization stage.

nudges.text: Your Triton kernel has a memory access fault likely from incorrect pointer arithmetic or shape mismatch. Instead of replacing the entire computation with one complex kernel, try an incremental approach: start with `torch.compile(mode="default")` on your working PyTorch forward pass to get an easy speedup, then selectively fuse smaller pieces (e.g., the segsum+exp which accounts for ~13% of time) with simple Triton kernels. Your working baseline scores 60 — build on that incrementally rather than rewriting everything at once.

The `torch.compile` correctness failure is likely caused by `einops.rearrange` not being properly traced by torch.compile. Replace all `rearrange` calls with equivalent native PyTorch ops (`reshape`, `permute`, `view`, `transpose`) in your forward function before applying `torch.compile`. For example, `rearrange(A, "b (c l) h -> b c l h", l=block_len)` is equivalent to `A.view(b, c, block_len, h)`.
